title: oxalis priceae subsp. colorea
intro: Oxalis priceae subsp. colorea, commonly known as the yellow wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The therapeutic actions of Oxalis priceae subsp. colorea are attributed to its bioactive constituents, which include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, that have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, cytotoxic, and immunomodulatory activities. Furthermore, research has identified the presence of oxalic acid, which contributes to its therapeutic actions, making it a valuable plant for the development of new medicines.
